notes:
Opening opportunity work notes, plaintiff.

Objective: opening statement for proposition that by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace under the market rule.

Record inspection: get_case and list_evidence show only five system case-packet files: ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page, ev_9f8061d601c0_primary-evidence, ev_72c545ccb705_official-source-record, ev_4a5f41c923ac_secondary-reporting, ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps. No party-submitted evidence yet. Read all five full contents via read_evidence_range. Market page preserves rule text and operative elements. Primary/official/secondary packet files say no merits packet has been built and counsel should find/submit public sources before relying on them. Unresolved gaps list missing NOTAM/authority records, missing reporting, missing UMA rationales, timing and breadth issues, and whether any closure was Iran-initiated rather than airline/foreign-government action.

Issue breakdown: plaintiff must prove by preponderance (1) Iran initiated the restriction, (2) it was non-weather, (3) by the May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET deadline, and (4) it was major under the rule: broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian region; or a non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports.

Native investigation/search log: web searches run: 'Iran airspace closed May 24 2026 flights suspended Iran aviation authority May 24 2026'; 'site:reuters.com Iran airspace closed May 24 2026 flights'; 'Iran Civil Aviation Organization May 24 2026 airspace closure NOTAM'; '"A0225/26" Iran NOTAM May 22 2026 western airspace'; '"western Tehran" "May 25" "NOTAM" Iran'; '"Iran" "A0225/26" "NOTAM"'. Leads found but not admitted: Polymarket page snippets and social/news snippets refer to a May 22-25 western Tehran FIR/airspace NOTAM; snippets mention possible OIIX A1010/26 and also later contradictory snippets saying no new NOTAM/restrictions lifted. These are leads only; not to be cited as record proof during opening.

Source map for later phases: strongest primary target is official Iranian aviation authority/CAA/CAO or NOTAM text for Tehran FIR/OIIX around May 22-25, 2026, plus airport authority operations notices for IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN. Corroborating sources: Reuters/AP/major aviation outlets, SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP operational summaries, flight-tracker observations with caution. Adverse checks: partial/regional or nighttime-only limits; permit exceptions; airline/foreign-state avoidance instead of Iranian closure; timing after May 24 ET; later official corrections or denials.

Reasoning/draft theory: opening should rely on admitted packet only for rule and gaps, not claim the outside leads as established. Plaintiff theory is that official/NOTAM evidence, if admitted, will likely decide the case: a security/military Iranian restriction that suspended civil flight permissions across a major Iranian region and affected multiple listed airports before deadline would satisfy the market rule even with narrow prior-permission exceptions. Defense will argue the packet currently lacks primary proof and that a western/regional/daylight/permit restriction is too limited to be 'major.'

Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with record contents, search terms, leads, adverse checks, and source plan.

Opening defendant notes: Record checked with get_case/list_evidence. Visible record contains only five system case-packet files (market-page, primary-evidence, official-source-record, secondary-reporting, unresolved-record-gaps) plus plaintiff opening; no exhibits or submitted merits evidence. Stat/read performed for all five packet files. Packet establishes market rule: major closure requires broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, or complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Packet excludes limited cancellations/delays/partial closures, airline/country warnings, weather. Packet explicitly says no merits official-source, secondary-reporting, NOTAM, airport operations, or flight-tracking evidence has been built. Unresolved gaps: official records, NOTAMs, airport operations, whether changes were Iran-initiated, breadth, causation, and exact ET timing.

Source map: decisive elements are Iranian initiation, non-weather cause, timing by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET, major breadth, and provenance. Best sources: official CAO/CAA announcement or attributable NOTAM, airport ops for listed airports, credible reporting, cautious flight tracking. Adverse sources: foreign/operator warnings only; partial regional airport restrictions; exception/permission regimes; source-chain breaks.

External investigation for opening theory only, not record proof: searched web for May 24 2026 Iran airspace NOTAM and OIIX A1010/26. Leads: Notamify appears to preserve raw NOTAM A1010/26 text with B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST and wording that all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except specified airports, with new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights on exceptions. Indian Express May 23 report says Iranian CAA issued OIIX A1010/26 closing western Tehran FIR airports and attributes to Gulf News. SafeAirspace gives later June context that OIIX partly reopened/west remained closed. These must be admitted in later phase before reliance. Potential conflicts: raw exception list seems not to match Indian Express's list of operational airports; raw NOTAM seems airport-operations focused rather than necessarily a full airspace transit closure. Defense opening should stress burden and rule application, not assert unadmitted facts as proof.

Arguments opportunity work notes, plaintiff.

Record recheck: get_case/list_evidence after defense opening showed no newly submitted evidence; record includes only packet evidence and openings. Defendant opening argues burden, lack of primary source chain, and partial/regional/daylight/permit restrictions.

Decisive elements: (1) Iran initiated; (2) non-weather; (3) by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; (4) major closure under market rule. Rule evidence remains ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page. Known gaps remain ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps.

Native investigation performed: opened accessible source pages from search leads. Gulf News page was accessible and captured page text: https://gulfnews.com/world/mena/iran-shuts-western-airspace-amid-fears-of-renewed-us-strikes-1.500550862. Indian Express page was accessible and captured page text: https://indianexpress.com/article/world/us-news/iran-closes-airspace-trump-cancels-plans-us-military-strikes-rubio-india-10704692/. Also checked Polymarket current event page and operational background pages OPSGROUP and SafeAirspace. Polymarket contains market context and summaries but packet already preserves rule; not submitted as merits source. OPSGROUP/SafeAirspace mostly discuss later/ongoing restrictions and foreign operator advice; not directly probative of May 22-25 initiating event, so not submitted.

Evidence submitted and accepted: ev_fcd4dae9c0c1_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-fcd4dae9c0c1 (Gulf News capture) and ev_cc708d7ea2db_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-cc708d7ea2db (Indian Express capture). Gulf News reports Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary closure over western Iran, all civil flight permits suspended at airports in western Tehran FIR sector, only eight airports operational including Mehrabad, Imam Khomeini, Isfahan, Yazd, and daylight/per-flight CAA approval restrictions. Indian Express reports OIIX A1010/26 NOTAM issued May 22, effective immediately until May 25, shutting all airports in western Tehran FIR, western half closure, only eight airports operational including THR, IKA, IFN, SYZ, MHD, plus Yazd, Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, daylight-only and per-flight CAA approval.

Adverse/limits: underlying official NOTAM was not captured; secondary source chain, Indian Express relies partly on Gulf News though it adds identifier/details. Airport-specific prong should not be overstated as complete suspension for IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD because evidence says those airports remained operational only daylight/per-approval. Stronger argument is broad major Iranian airspace region: western half/western sector of Tehran FIR, all airports in western part shut, civil permits suspended, from May 22 to May 25, non-weather security/military rationale. Limited exceptions and approval regime do not disqualify under rule; they show ordinary commercial operations were suspended absent CAA approval.

Planned filing: offer market rule packet evidence plus two new source captures; argue preponderance satisfied by specific, corroborated reporting of Iran-initiated NOTAM/CAA action before deadline. Include source-chain caveat and why it affects weight but not enough to defeat preponderance.

Arguments defendant notes: Record rechecked with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff added Gulf News ev_fcd4dae9c0c1 and Indian Express ev_cc708d7ea2db. Exact reads performed for both. Gulf News reports CAO announcement temporarily closing airspace over western Iran; civil flight permits suspended at airports in western sector of Tehran FIR; eight airports operational incl Mehrabad/Imam Khomeini/Isfahan/Yazd; daylight-only and per-flight approval. Indian Express reports NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 issued May 22, effective until May 25, all airports in western Tehran FIR shut, eight operational airports incl Mehrabad, Imam Khomeini, Isfahan, Shiraz, Yazd, Tabriz, Mashhad, Bandar Abbas; rerouting.

Further investigation: opened Notamify OIIX A1010/26 page https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/693f2d0f-54d3-433b-8128-49f5475ba269. Submitted capture accepted as ev_2e5195da64cb_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-2e5195da64cb. Raw text: A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/; A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST; E) REF NOTAM A0796/26, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational sunrise-sunset; previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports.

Analysis: Timing covers cutoff (B 2026-05-22 20:46 UTC; C 2026-05-25 08:30 estimated UTC). Non-weather/security context plausible. Iranian initiation plausible via CAA permission language and secondary reports. Defense issue is breadth. Raw NOTAM is airport-operations QFALC/AD, not enroute airspace. Text closes airports in west part of Tehran FIR, not all commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from a major airspace region. Exceptions remain operational HJ with permission. Plaintiff's airport-specific path is not clean: raw exception list differs from Indian Express list and does not establish complete suspension at at least two listed airports. Official Iranian source/official NOTAM distribution and referenced A0796/26 remain missing. Merits argument should concede what record supports but say plaintiff has not met the rule's major-closure threshold by preponderance.

Rebuttal opportunity work notes, plaintiff.

Record/evidence scan: list_evidence shows new defendant submitted evidence ev_2e5195da64cb_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-2e5195da64cb, a Notamify capture of OIIX A1010/26. Read full 1515 bytes and stat metadata. No need for further outside source; defendant's raw NOTAM capture is strongest evidence to answer.

Defendant argument: raw NOTAM is airport operations/QFALC, not airspace closure; airport-specific path not proven; exceptions/daylight/per-permission mean partial; no official source.

Exact NOTAM content read: A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q) OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX; B)2605222046 C)2605250830 EST; E)REF NOTAM A0796/26, ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD EXCEPT OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, AND OIAW THAT ARE OPERATIONAL FM SUNRISE TO SUNSET (HJ). ALL PREVIOUS PERMISSIONS ARE SUSPENDED FOR ALL OPERATORS, NEW PERMISSION SHALL BE REQUESTED FM CAA FOR OPRATION OF CIVIL IFR PASSENGER FLIGHTS ON THESE AIRPORTS.

Rebuttal analysis: Use the raw text rather than fight it. It confirms the source-chain core: NOTAM, OIIX/Tehran FIR, issued/effective 2026-05-22 20:46 UTC through 2026-05-25 08:30 UTC estimated (covers deadline), all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports, previous permissions suspended for all operators, new CAA permission needed for civil IFR passenger flights. This supports Iranian initiation, timing, non-weather context via plaintiff reports, and major-region commercial arrival/departure suspension. Defendant's QFALC/airport-operations point narrows transit/overflight but does not defeat rule because the rule includes arriving/departing commercial flights in a major airspace region and allows limited exceptions. We should not rely on airport-specific complete-suspension prong; use broad major-region path. Exceptions are limited and conditional, so under rule they do not disqualify.

No new evidence submitted. Planned rebuttal offered evidence: defendant NOTAM capture plus plaintiff source reports and market rule.

Surrebuttal notes: Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence after defendant merits; plaintiff rebuttal relies on defendant Notamify capture ev_2e5195da64cb plus existing reports. Stat/read performed on ev_2e5195da64cb in this opportunity. Plaintiff argues Notamify supplies missing source-chain link and confirms timely Iran-controlled major-region civil passenger-flight suspension with limited exceptions; says rule is not limited to en-route overflight and airport closures plus daylight/permission exceptions satisfy major-region path.

Defense response: raw NOTAM remains classified AD Airport Operations with Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A scope. It closes airports in west part of Tehran FIR except eight operational sunrise-sunset with new CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights on those airports. It does not say the FIR/airspace or enroute/transit operations are closed generally. The rule's first path requires broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or major airspace region. Plaintiff proves only an airport-operations restriction, not transit/overflight or general region-wide commercial flight closure. The limited-exception clause applies only once a broad closure is established; it cannot convert a partial airport NOTAM into a broad closure. Airport prong is the natural fit but plaintiff abandons it and has not proven complete suspension at two listed airports.

Closing opportunity work notes, plaintiff.

Final record/evidence scan: get_case/list_evidence show no new evidence after defendant surrebuttal. Evidence remains packet files, plaintiff Gulf News and Indian Express captures, defendant Notamify raw NOTAM capture. Defendant surrebuttal added no evidence; it relies on QFALC/AD Airport Operations and argues airport restrictions are not major airspace-region closure.

Closing assessment: Strong plaintiff facts are market rule's major-region path and limited-exceptions clause; Notamify raw NOTAM confirms all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except eight, all previous permissions suspended for all operators, new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights, and effective 2026-05-22 20:46 UTC through 2026-05-25 08:30 EST, covering the deadline. Gulf News and Indian Express provide Iranian CAO/CAA attribution and military/security, non-weather context; Indian Express identifies same NOTAM.

Concessions/limits: Official Iranian artifact and referenced A0796/26 missing. Airport-specific complete-suspension-at-two-listed-airports path is weak and should not be central. Closing should frame burden as preponderance and argue the major-region arrival/departure commercial-flight suspension is enough under rule, even if NOTAM classification is airport operations and even with limited daylight/CAA-permission exceptions.

Planned closing: concise synthesis; confront defense's strongest point (QFALC/airport operations); emphasize that the rule covers arriving/departing commercial flights in major region, not only overflight/transit, and expressly allows limited CAA-approved exceptions.

Closing notes: Record scanned with list_evidence in closing. Same evidence set: case packet files, plaintiff Gulf News ev_fcd4dae9c0c1, plaintiff Indian Express ev_cc708d7ea2db, defendant Notamify raw NOTAM ev_2e5195da64cb. Stat/read performed for market-page and Notamify in closing opportunity. Plaintiff closing argues exact notice plus rule proves major-region commercial-flight suspension with limited exceptions.

Closing theory: concede record likely proves timely, non-weather/security, Iran/CAA-linked airport restriction. Decisive failure is breadth under market rule. Raw NOTAM, even taken at face value, is Notamify capture rather than official source and is classified AD Airport Operations/QFALC/A; text closes airports in west part of Tehran FIR, not the FIR/airspace/enroute transit generally. Plaintiff's interpretation rewrites airport operations into airspace region commercial flight closure. Limited-exception clause only applies once broad closure exists; cannot override partial-closure exclusion. Airport-specific path not proven for two listed airports and plaintiff largely abandons it. Official A0796/26 and official distribution still missing.
